papers.summary: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures are increasingly popular for frontier large language models (LLM) but they introduce training challenges due to routing complexity. Fully leveraging parameters of an MoE model requires all experts to be well-trained and to specialize in non-redundant ways. papers.summary: In today's AI-assisted software engineering landscape, developers increasingly depend on LLMs that are highly capable, yet inherently imperfect. papers.summary_zh: 由於適應新的程式語言 (PL) 對需要複雜的工程工作，大多數儲存庫級別的程式碼翻譯與驗證技術僅針對單一來源-目標 PL 對進行過評估。程式設計代理可以實現在儲存庫級別程式碼翻譯與驗證中的 PL 無關性：它們可以跨多種 PL 生成程式碼，並自主使用針對每個 PL 分析的現有工具。然而，最先進的技術尚未提供一種完全自主的代理方法。

papers.summary_zh: 命題線性時態邏輯 (LTL) 是一種流行的形式化方法，用於指定軟體、網路和系統的所需需求以及安全與隱私策略。然而，由於其複雜的語義，以 LTL 表達此類需求和策略仍然具有挑戰性。由於許多安全和隱私分析工具需要 LTL 公式作為輸入，這種困難使得許多開發人員和分析師無法使用它們。大型語言模型 (LLMs) 有望擴大此類工具的普及度。

papers.summary_zh: 語用推理（Pragmatic inference）本質上是分級的。不同的詞彙項目（lexical items）會產生不同程度的語用豐富（pragmatic enrichment）。標量蘊涵（Scalar implicature）透過標量多樣性（scalar diversity）來例證此特性，其中蘊涵強度（implicature strength）在不同的標量項目之間變化。然而，對 Large Language Models (LLMs) 中語用推理的評估通常依賴於基於 prompt 的操作（manipulations）。除了 prompt 層次的效應，本研究引入了 Continuous Interpretive Steering (CIS)，這是一種探測分級語用 inter

primary_link.title: STQuant: Spatio-Temporal Adaptive Framework for Optimizer Quantization in Large Multimodal Model Training

papers.summary: Quantization is an effective way to reduce the memory cost of large-scale model training. primary_link.title: When Is Thinking Enough? Early Exit via Sufficiency Assessment for Efficient Reasoning

papers.summary: Large reasoning models (LRMs) have achieved remarkable performance in complex reasoning tasks, driven by their powerful inference-time scaling capability. papers.summary_zh: Large reasoning models (LRMs) 憑藉其強大的 inference-time 擴展能力，在複雜推理任務中取得了卓越的性能。然而，LRMs 經常面臨過度思考的問題，這會導致大量的計算冗餘並顯著降低效率。Early-exit 方法旨在透過在生成足夠證據後終止推理來緩解這個問題，但現有方法大多依賴不可靠的手工或經驗性指標。

papers.summary: Visual in-context learning models are designed to adapt to new tasks by leveraging a set of example input-output pairs, enabling rapid generalization without task-specific fine-tuning. However, these models operate in a fundamentally static paradigm: while they can adapt to new tasks, they lack any mechanism to incorporate user-provided guidance signals such as scribbles, clicks, or bounding boxes to steer or refine the prediction process. radar.summary_zh: 儘管 Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) 已取得顯著進展，但由於其依賴 2D 視覺先驗，在複雜的 3D 空間推理方面仍面臨挑戰。現有方法通常透過在有限的 3D 資料集上進行計算成本高昂的後訓練程序，或透過缺乏明確幾何理解和視角靈活性的僵化 tool-calling 機制來緩解此限制。為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了一個 training-free 框架。

radar.summary_zh: 背景：Large Language Models (LLMs) 越來越多地應用於現代軟體開發，透過 AI 驅動的助手協助程式碼生成、程式碼補全和重構。儘管它們加速了開發流程，但經常產生額外的輸出，被稱為「喋喋不休」(babbling)，這會產生額外的認知、經濟和能源成本。目標：本研究調查基於 LLM 的程式碼生成中「喋喋不休」的問題，並提出一種實用且 model-agnostic 的方法來減少。

radar.summary_zh: Large Chunk Test-Time Training (LaCT) 在長上下文 3D 重建方面表現出色，但其完全可塑的推論時更新仍然容易受到災難性遺忘和過度擬合的影響。因此，LaCT 通常以一個涵蓋完整輸入序列的單一大型 chunk 進行實例化，未能實現一次處理任意長序列的更廣泛目標。我們提出受 elastic weight consolidation 啟發的 Elastic Test-Time Training，它能穩定。

radar.summary: Recent advances in vision-language models (VLMs) have improved image captioning for cultural heritage. We introduce a multi-category, cross-cultural benchmark for this task and evaluate VLMs using an LLM-as-Judge framework that measures semantic alignment with reference annotations. To assess cultural reasoning, we report exact-match, partial-match, and attribute-level accurac
radar.summary_zh: vision-language models (VLMs) 的最新進展改善了文化遺產的圖像描述。然而，從視覺輸入推斷結構化文化元資料（例如，創作者、起源、時期）仍未得到充分探索。我們為此任務引入了一個多類別、跨文化的基準，並使用 LLM-as-Judge 框架評估 VLMs，該框架衡量與參考註釋的語義對齊程度。為了評估文化推理能力，我們報告了 exact-match、partial-match 和 attribute-level 準確度。
radar.title: Appear2Meaning: A Cross-Cultural Benchmark for Structured Cultural Metadata Inference from Images
radar.title_zh: Appear2Meaning：用於從圖像推斷結構化文化元資料的跨文化基準

links.title: Critical Inker: Scaffolding Critical Thinking in AI-Assisted Writing Through Socratic Questioning

primary_link.title: Critical Inker: Scaffolding Critical Thinking in AI-Assisted Writing Through Socratic Questioning

radar.summary: As Large Language Models (LLMs) increasingly automate writing tasks, there is a growing risk of cognitive deskilling where users offload critical thinking to the system. radar.summary_zh: 隨著 Large Language Models (LLMs) 越來越多地自動化寫作任務，認知去技能化 (cognitive deskilling) 的風險日益增加，即用戶將批判性思考轉嫁給系統。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了 Critical Inker，這是一個旨在透過邏輯分析和蘇格拉底式回饋，在寫作過程中協助批判性反思的寫作工具。我們提出了兩種方法：(1) 一個蘇格拉底式 chatbot，利用問題幫助使用者意識並修正寫作中的邏輯錯誤，以及 (2) Visual Feedback，其突出顯示
radar.title: Critical Inker: Scaffolding Critical Thinking in AI-Assisted Writing Through Socratic Questioning
radar.title_zh: Critical Inker：透過蘇格拉底式提問在AI輔助寫作中培養批判性思考

links.title: AEROS: A Single-Agent Operating Architecture with Embodied Capability Modules

primary_link.title: AEROS: A Single-Agent Operating Architecture with Embodied Capability Modules

radar.summary: Robotic systems lack a principled abstraction for organizing intelligence, capabilities, and execution in a unified manner. Existing approaches either couple skills within monolithic architectures or decompose functionality into loosely coordinated modules or multiple agents, often without a coherent model of identity and control authority. radar.summary_zh: 機器人系統缺乏一種原則性的抽象來統一組織智慧、能力和執行。現有方法要麼將技能耦合在 monolithic architectures 中，要麼將功能分解為 loosely coordinated modules 或 multiple agents，而通常缺乏連貫的身份和控制權模型。我們認為，機器人應被建模為一個單一持久智能主體，其能力可透過 installable packages 進行擴展。我們將其形式化
radar.title: AEROS: A Single-Agent Operating Architecture with Embodied Capability Modules
radar.title_zh: AEROS：一種具備具身能力模組的單代理操作架構

links.title: Strategic Persuasion with Trait-Conditioned Multi-Agent Systems for Iterative Legal Argumentation

primary_link.title: Strategic Persuasion with Trait-Conditioned Multi-Agent Systems for Iterative Legal Argumentation

radar.summary: Strategic interaction in adversarial domains such as law, diplomacy, and negotiation is mediated by language, yet most game-theoretic models abstract away the mechanisms of persuasion that operate through discourse. radar.title: Strategic Persuasion with Trait-Conditioned Multi-Agent Systems for Iterative Legal Argumentation
radar.title_zh: 運用特徵條件式多代理系統進行疊代式法律論證的策略性說服

links.title: EmoMAS: Emotion-Aware Multi-Agent System for High-Stakes Edge-Deployable Negotiation with Bayesian Orchestration

primary_link.title: EmoMAS: Emotion-Aware Multi-Agent System for High-Stakes Edge-Deployable Negotiation with Bayesian Orchestration

radar.summary: Large language models (LLMs) has been widely used for automated negotiation, but their high computational cost and privacy risks limit deployment in privacy-sensitive, on-device settings such as mobile assistants or rescue robots. Small language models (SLMs) offer a viable alternative, yet struggle with the complex emotional dynamics of high-stakes negotiation. radar.summary_zh: Large language models (LLMs) 已被廣泛用於自動化談判，但其高昂的計算成本和隱私風險限制了它們在 privacy-sensitive, on-device settings（例如 mobile assistants 或 rescue robots）中的部署。Small language models (SLMs) 提供了一個可行的替代方案，但在 high-stakes negotiation 的複雜情緒動態方面表現不佳。我們引入了 EmoMAS，這是一個 Bayesian multi-agent framework，它將情緒決策從 reactive 轉變為 strategic。EmoMAS 利用
radar.title: EmoMAS: Emotion-Aware Multi-Agent System for High-Stakes Edge-Deployable Negotiation with Bayesian Orchestration
radar.title_zh: EmoMAS：用於高風險邊緣部署談判並結合貝葉斯協調的情緒感知多代理系統

links.title: iTAG: Inverse Design for Natural Text Generation with Accurate Causal Graph Annotations

primary_link.title: iTAG: Inverse Design for Natural Text Generation with Accurate Causal Graph Annotations

radar.summary: A fundamental obstacle to causal discovery from text is the lack of causally annotated text data for use as ground truth, due to high annotation costs. This motivates an important task of generating text with causal graph annotations. Early template-based generation methods sacrifice text naturalness in exchange for high causal graph annotation accuracy. radar.summary_zh: 從文本中進行因果發現的一個根本障礙是，由於高昂的註解成本，缺乏可作為 ground truth 的帶有因果註解的文本數據。這激發了一項重要任務：生成帶有因果圖註解的文本。早期的 template-based generation methods 犧牲了文本自然度，以換取高因果圖註解準確性。最近依賴 Large Language Model (LLM) 的方法透過 LLMs 直接從目標圖生成自然文本，但不保證因果
radar.title: iTAG: Inverse Design for Natural Text Generation with Accurate Causal Graph Annotations
radar.title_zh: iTAG：用於生成具有精確因果圖註解的自然文本的逆向設計

links.title: HingeMem: Boundary Guided Long-Term Memory with Query Adaptive Retrieval for Scalable Dialogues

primary_link.title: HingeMem: Boundary Guided Long-Term Memory with Query Adaptive Retrieval for Scalable Dialogues

radar.summary: Long-term memory is critical for dialogue systems that support continuous, sustainable, and personalized interactions. However, existing methods rely on continuous summarization or OpenIE-based graph construction paired with fixed Top-\textit{k} retrieval, leading to limited adaptability across query categories and high computational overhead. In this paper, we propose HingeMem, a boundary-guided long-term memory that operationalizes event segmentation theory to build an interpretable indexing i
radar.summary_zh: 長期記憶對於支援連續、可持續和個性化互動的對話系統至關重要。然而，現有方法依賴於連續摘要或基於 OpenIE 的圖形構建，並搭配固定的 Top-\textit{k} 檢索，導致查詢類別之間的適應性有限且計算開銷高。在本文中，我們提出了 HingeMem，一種邊界引導的長期記憶，它運用事件分割理論來構建可解釋的索引
radar.title: HingeMem: Boundary Guided Long-Term Memory with Query Adaptive Retrieval for Scalable Dialogues
radar.title_zh: HingeMem: 邊界引導的長期記憶與查詢適應性檢索用於可擴展對話

links.title: Evaluating LLM-Based 0-to-1 Software Generation in End-to-End CLI Tool Scenarios

primary_link.title: Evaluating LLM-Based 0-to-1 Software Generation in End-to-End CLI Tool Scenarios

radar.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are driving a shift towards intent-driven development, where agents build complete software from scratch. However, existing benchmarks fail to assess this 0-to-1 generation capability due to two limitations: reliance on predefined scaffolds that ignore repository structure planning, and rigid white-box unit testing that lacks end-to-end behavioral validation. To bridge this gap, we introduce CLI-Tool-Bench, a structure-agnostic benchmark for evaluating the ground-up
radar.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 正在推動朝向意圖驅動開發的轉變，在這種開發中，代理從零開始構建完整的軟體。然而，現有基準測試未能評估這種從零到一的生成能力，原因有兩個限制：一是依賴預定義的 scaffolds 而忽略了 repository 結構規劃，二是僵化的 white-box 單元測試缺乏端到端行為驗證。為了彌補這一鴻溝，我們引入了 CLI-Tool-Bench，這是一個與結構無關的基準測試，用於評估從底層開始的
radar.title: Evaluating LLM-Based 0-to-1 Software Generation in End-to-End CLI Tool Scenarios
radar.title_zh: 在端到端 CLI Tool 情境中評估基於 LLM 的從零到一軟體生成

links.title: TEC: A Collection of Human Trial-and-error Trajectories for Problem Solving

primary_link.title: TEC: A Collection of Human Trial-and-error Trajectories for Problem Solving

radar.summary: Trial-and-error is a fundamental strategy for humans to solve complex problems and a necessary capability for Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems operating in real-world environments. Although several trial-and-error AI techniques have recently been proposed, most of them rely on simple heuristics designed by researchers and achieve limited performance gains. The core issue is the absence of appropriate data: current models cannot learn from detailed records of how humans actually conduct trial
radar.summary_zh: 試錯 (Trial-and-error) 是人類解決複雜問題的基本策略，也是在真實世界環境中運作的 Artificial Intelligence (AI) 系統的必要能力。儘管最近提出了幾種試錯 AI 技術，但其中大多數依賴於研究人員設計的簡單 heuristics，並且實現的性能提升有限。核心問題是缺乏適當的數據：目前的模型無法從人類實際進行試錯的詳細記錄中學習
radar.title: TEC: A Collection of Human Trial-and-error Trajectories for Problem Solving
radar.title_zh: TEC: 一個人類試錯軌跡集合用於問題解決

links.title: Beyond Pessimism: Offline Learning in KL-regularized Games

primary_link.title: Beyond Pessimism: Offline Learning in KL-regularized Games

radar.summary: We study offline learning in KL-regularized two-player zero-sum games, where policies are optimized under a KL constraint to a fixed reference policy. Prior work relies on pessimistic value estimation to handle distribution shift, yielding only $\widetilde{\mathcal{O}}(1/\sqrt n)$ statistical rates. radar.summary_zh: 我們研究了 KL-regularized 雙人零和博弈中的離線學習，其中策略是在 KL 限制下對固定參考策略進行優化。先前的工作依賴於悲觀的價值估計來處理分佈偏移 (distribution shift)，僅產生 $\widetilde{\mathcal{O}}(1/\sqrt n)$ 的統計速率。我們為 KL-regularized 博弈開發了一種新的無悲觀算法和分析框架，其基礎是 KL-regularized 最佳響應的平滑性和 Nash equilibrium 的穩定性
radar.title: Beyond Pessimism: Offline Learning in KL-regularized Games
radar.title_zh: 超越悲觀主義：KL-regularized 博弈中的離線學習

radar.summary_zh: 深度神經網路可以預測人類判斷，但這並不意味著它們依賴於類人信息或揭示了這些判斷的潛在線索。先前的工作使用 attribution heatmaps 解決了這個問題，但其解釋價值本身取決於 robustness。在此，我們通過評估預測人類真實性評級的模型是否也能在內部和跨架構產生一致的解釋來測試這些解釋的 robustness。我們擬合了 lightweight regr

links.title: Designing for Accountable Agents: a Viewpoint

primary_link.title: Designing for Accountable Agents: a Viewpoint

radar.summary: AI systems are becoming increasingly complex, ubiquitous and autonomous, leading to increasing concerns about their impacts on individuals and society. In response, researchers have begun investigating how to ensure that the methods underlying AI decision-making are transparent and their decisions are explainable to people and conformant to human values and ethical principles. radar.summary_zh: AI 系統日益複雜、無所不在且自主，這引發了人們對其對個人和社會影響的日益關注。為此，研究人員已開始調查如何確保 AI 決策所依據的方法是透明的，其決策對人們而言是可解釋的，並符合人類價值觀和道德原則。作為這項研究重點的一部分，AI 系統中對 accountability 的需求已被提出，但這個概念已被證明...

links.title: Walk the Talk: Bridging the Reasoning-Action Gap for Thinking with Images via Multimodal Agentic Policy Optimization

primary_link.title: Walk the Talk: Bridging the Reasoning-Action Gap for Thinking with Images via Multimodal Agentic Policy Optimization

top5.summary: Recent advancements in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have incentivized models to ``think with images'' by actively invoking visual tools during multi-turn reasoning. The common Reinforcement Learning (RL) practice of relying on outcome-based rewards ignores the fact that textual plausibility often masks executive failure, meaning that models may exhibit intuitive textual reasoning while executing imprecise or irrelevant visual actions within their agentic reasoning trajectories. top5.summary_zh: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) 的最新進展促使模型透過在多輪推理期間主動調用視覺工具來「透過圖像思考」。常見的 Reinforcement Learning (RL) 實踐依賴於基於結果的獎勵，卻忽略了文本合理性往往掩蓋執行失敗的事實，這意味著模型可能在智能體推理軌跡中展現直觀的文本推理，同時執行不精確或不相關的視覺動作。這

links.title: Geometric Properties of the Voronoi Tessellation in Latent Semantic Manifolds of Large Language Models

primary_link.title: Geometric Properties of the Voronoi Tessellation in Latent Semantic Manifolds of Large Language Models

top5.summary: Language models operate on discrete tokens but compute in continuous vector spaces, inducing a Voronoi tessellation over the representation manifold. We study this tessellation empirically on Qwen3.5-4B-Base, making two contributions. top5.summary_zh: 語言模型在離散的 token 上操作，但在連續向量空間中計算，這在表示流形上誘導了 Voronoi 鑲嵌。我們在 Qwen3.5-4B-Base 上經驗性地研究了這種鑲嵌，並做出了兩項貢獻。首先，使用 float32 邊距重新計算來解決 bfloat16 量化偽影，我們驗證了 Mabrok (2026) 的表現力差距線性擴展定律，其 $R^2$ = 0.9997 — 迄今為止最強的確認 — 並識別出一個中間層的幾何模糊性。

links.title: InfiniLoRA: Disaggregated Multi-LoRA Serving for Large Language Models

primary_link.title: InfiniLoRA: Disaggregated Multi-LoRA Serving for Large Language Models

top5.summary: LoRA enables efficient customization of LLMs and is widely used in multi-tenant and multi-task serving. However, emerging model architectures such as MoE significantly increase LoRA memory cost, making existing coupled LoRA serving designs poorly scalable and prone to tail-latency inflation. links.title: Scheduling the Unschedulable: Taming Black-Box LLM Inference at Scale

primary_link.title: Scheduling the Unschedulable: Taming Black-Box LLM Inference at Scale

top5.summary: When output token counts can be predicted at submission time (Gan et al., 2026), client-side scheduling against a black-box LLM API becomes semi-clairvoyant: decisions condition on coarse token priors even though the provider's internals remain hidden. We decompose this boundary problem into three separable concerns: allocation (inter-class share via adaptive DRR), ordering (intra-class sequencing with feasible-set scoring), and overload control (explicit admit/defer/reject on a cost ladder). top5.summary_zh: 當輸出 token 數量可以在提交時預測時 (Gan et al., 2026)，針對黑箱 LLM API 的客戶端調度變成了半預知：決策基於粗略的 token 先驗，儘管提供者的內部機制保持隱藏。我們將這個邊界問題分解為三個可分離的關注點：分配（透過自適應 DRR 進行類間共享）、排序（透過可行集評分進行類內排序）和過載控制（在成本階梯上明確的接納/延遲/拒絕）。一個

links.title: Beyond End-to-End: Dynamic Chain Optimization for Private LLM Adaptation on the Edge

primary_link.title: Beyond End-to-End: Dynamic Chain Optimization for Private LLM Adaptation on the Edge

top5.summary: Federated fine-tuning enables privacy-preserving LLM adaptation but faces a critical bottleneck: the disparity between LLMs' high memory demands and edge devices' limited capacity. To break the memory barrier, we propose Chain Federated Fine-Tuning (ChainFed), an innovative paradigm that forgoes end-to-end updates in favor of a sequential, layer-by-layer manner.
